title: fix: optimize semantic recall latency across all storage adapters

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(pg): replace ROW_NUMBER() with cursor-based queries for semantic recall
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relationship: predecessor — this PR extends that pg-only fix to all remaining adapters

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: feat(pg): add btree indexes on memory_messages metadata fields
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relationship: complementary — adds indexes that this PR's cursor queries benefit from

summary: Optimizes semantic recall latency across all 11 storage adapters (pg, libsql, cloudflare-d1, mssql, mongodb, dynamodb, upstash, clickhouse, convex, lance, cloudflare). The core change replaces slow ROW_NUMBER() window functions and correlated subselects in _getIncludedMessages() with a two-phase approach: batch-fetch target message metadata in one query, then use cursor-based pagination with materialized constants. Also adds perPage=0 early exits to skip unnecessary COUNT(*) and data queries on the semantic recall path. Includes a performance test and 4 changesets.
maintainerNotes: Approved by wardpeet. CI is mostly green — the one failure is Observability Package Tests which is unrelated to storage/memory changes. Memory Tests, Combined store Tests, and E2E Tests are still pending at triage time. The PR is large (17 files, 1789 lines changed) but tightly scoped: every changed file is a memory/index.ts in a store adapter, applying the same pattern. This extends the proven pg fix from PR #11365 to all other adapters. CodeRabbit left one minor nitpick about O(n²) cache scanning in the Convex adapter but otherwise approved. Should be safe to merge once the pending checks complete successfully.
